location: Pasir Ris Wafer Fab Park, Singapore
description: UMC announced a new 22/28nm wafer fab expansion in Singapore to support specialty technologies for 5G, automotive, and IoT applications.
why_it_matters: It proves UMC already has operational familiarity, local government relationships, and customer confidence in Singapore as a high-value foundry location.
strategic_lesson_for_umc: Use Singapore for high-assurance manufacturing and regional commercial control, but apply strict capex discipline because cost levels are high.

description: Intel announced a major Malaysia investment to expand advanced packaging, assembly, and test capacity.
why_it_matters: The investment reinforces Malaysia's role as a regional back-end semiconductor and advanced packaging platform.
strategic_lesson_for_umc: Malaysia is the most credible ASEAN market for UMC to explore packaging partnerships, OSAT adjacency, and staged manufacturing options.

description: GlobalFoundries opened a Singapore fab expansion adding 300mm semiconductor manufacturing capacity.
why_it_matters: It demonstrates that Singapore can still attract large foundry investments where infrastructure quality and customer assurance justify premium cost.
strategic_lesson_for_umc: Singapore is strongest when the strategic goal is reliable execution and customer confidence rather than lowest-cost capacity.

description: Infineon expanded its Kulim power semiconductor footprint, including SiC-related manufacturing capacity.
why_it_matters: Power semiconductor investment validates Malaysia's industrial infrastructure, utilities planning, and engineering workforce for high-value semiconductor manufacturing.
strategic_lesson_for_umc: Malaysia can support manufacturing-adjacent semiconductor expansion, but UMC should still gate foundry capex on utilities, talent, and anchor demand.

investment: Placeholder - update with verified project amount
description: ASE has continued to expand semiconductor packaging and testing operations in Penang.
why_it_matters: ASE's presence strengthens Penang's OSAT cluster and supplier base for back-end semiconductor services.
strategic_lesson_for_umc: UMC should evaluate partnership with OSAT or local suppliers in Malaysia before committing to owned advanced packaging capacity.

description: Amkor opened an advanced system-in-package assembly and test facility in Bac Ninh.
why_it_matters: The investment signals Vietnam's rising relevance in advanced back-end semiconductor supply chains.
strategic_lesson_for_umc: Vietnam is useful as an option-value market for support and future back-end partnerships, but less ready for immediate front-end foundry investment.
